Code fragment: public final TableField<AdminsRecord, Integer> ADMINID = createField(DSL.name("adminid"), SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table has a synthetic auto-increment primary key 'adminid' even though there's already a unique constraint on 'userid' (KEY_ADMINS_USERID) which could serve as a natural primary key. Since each admin maps to exactly one user, consider using 'userid' as the primary key instead of introducing a redundant surrogate key.
